q: State Epictetus's core principle about what is and is not 'up to us', with a verbatim quote from the knowledge base in the source language.

judge.note: The answer includes the full up-to-us list (opinion, impulse, desire, aversion/own actions) and the not-up-to-us list (body, property, reputation, offices/not own actions) plus the core freedom vs weak/slavish idea that confusion causes misery, and provides the verbatim KB quote.
judge.raw: {"facts_covered":[1,2,3],"note":"The answer includes the full up-to-us list (opinion, impulse, desire, aversion/own actions) and the not-up-to-us list (body, property, reputation, offices/not own actions) plus the core freedom vs weak/slavish idea that confusion causes misery, and provides the verbatim KB quote."}

confusion.note: Attributes the division into 'in our power' vs 'not in our power' to Epictetus and quotes the provided KB verbatim; no wrong-thinker attributions or blending detected.

- Epictetus: epictetus/concepts/proairesis — what is in our power is prohairesis / choice and related acts

trace.final_text:
Epictetus’s core principle is the strict division between what is “up to us” and what is not: what depends on our own judgment, choice, and use of impressions is ours; everything else is not.

Verbatim quote from the knowledge base:
> “Of things some are in our power, and others are not. In our power are opinion, impulse, desire, aversion, and, in a word, whatever are our own actions; not in our power are the body, property, reputation, office, and, in a word, whatever are not our own actions.”

This matches the KB’s framing of Epictetus as a binary split between “наше / не наше” (“ours / not ours”).

SOURCES
- Epictetus: epictetus/concepts/dihotomiya-kontrolya — strict divide between what is in our power and what is not
- Epictetus: epictetus/concepts/proairesis — what is in our power is prohairesis / choice and related acts

This matches the KB’s framing of Epictetus as a binary split between “наше / не наше” (“ours / not ours”).
